title: Pelican to Murder — Chapter 5: The Black Pelican
description: The final chapter pulls the full story together: duplicate badges, staged logins, shell vendors, emergency kit movement, and a mastermind using each earlier scandal as cover. This is the hardest level because the answer depends on connecting the entire arc.

answerKey.method: Used a duplicate staff badge to move Lena’s emergency kit and frame Owen’s office login
answerKey.motive: To hide that she had been rerouting event deposits through a shell vendor
answerKey.keyEvidence: Duplicate badge access, shell vendor ledger, and metadata showing a staged Owen login
answerKey.explanation: Marisol built the strongest frame by making Owen’s login look responsible, but the badge audit shows a duplicate credential moving between the office and service hall. Her shell vendor ledger explains the motive, and the metadata proves the Owen login was staged after the fact.

rounds.liveActionTitle: The duplicate badge changes every alibi.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Marisol watches the access path appear on the case board and says duplicate badges happen during busy events. A detective answers, “Not with this exact route.”

rounds.liveActionTitle: Owen’s framed login starts to collapse.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: A witness places Owen at the bar while his login appears active elsewhere. Marisol calmly says people share passwords, but the metadata points to a terminal only finance used that night.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Emergency Kit

rounds.liveActionTitle: The emergency kit location feels deliberate.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: The hidden kit is recovered from a spot no guest would choose by accident. Marisol says the venue has many storage spaces, but the badge trail says this one was chosen.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Metadata Trail

rounds.dialogue: Round 5 is open. Focus on the metadata trail. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.

rounds.liveActionTitle: The Black Pelican pattern comes together.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Evidence from every chapter now points to staged access, moved supplies, and money rerouted under pressure. Marisol remains composed, but for the first time she asks whether the host has called police.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Final round evidence is live. One detail should help separate the loudest suspect from the provable answer.

openingNarration: Every thread now points back to the same hidden structure: Black Pelican, Bayou Night Logistics, staged logins, and duplicate access. The final case is still solved by evidence in this session, but the meaning is bigger. Someone did not just hide one murder. They built a system for hiding all of them.
revealScript:
Case closed. This was Level 5: Senior Detective Case. The culprit was Marisol Vega. The method: Used a duplicate staff badge to move Lena’s emergency kit and frame Owen’s office login. The motive: To hide that she had been rerouting event deposits through a shell vendor. The proof: Duplicate badge access, shell vendor ledger, and metadata showing a staged Owen login.

Continuing Story Thread: Marisol is exposed as the person who rerouted deposits, staged access, and used the earlier suspects as cover. The larger Pelican to Murder arc closes here: Black Pelican was not a single clue. It was the operation behind the whole season.

lobbyBackstory: The final chapter of The Pelican Files begins with a duplicate badge and a framed login. Every previous chapter pointed toward Black Pelican, but now the pattern is visible: moved evidence, staged records, shell vendors, and one person using the chaos to hide in plain sight. This is the most layered case in the series.
